Name: Baseline
Noted Behavior: Stable baseline run. Agent starts near random performance and gradually improves over training.

Name: High LR
Noted Behavior: Updates are aggressive; reward tends to fluctuate and learning can become unstable.

Name: Low LR
Noted Behavior: Learning is slow and conservative; may require far more timesteps to reach strong performance.

Name: Low Gamma
Noted Behavior: Agent becomes short-sighted by prioritizing immediate rewards, often hurting long-term rally strategy.

Name: Medium Gamma
Noted Behavior: Balances immediate and future reward, usually better than very low gamma but often below 0.99 in Atari tasks.

Name: Large Batch
Noted Behavior: Gradient estimates are smoother and often more stable, but learning may progress slower early on.

Name: Small Batch
Noted Behavior: Noisier updates can improve early adaptation but often increase reward variance across episodes.

Name: Fast Epsilon Decay
Noted Behavior: Exploration ends quickly; agent may prematurely exploit weak strategies and plateau early.

Name: Slow Epsilon Decay
Noted Behavior: Long exploration phase improves state coverage, but reward gains usually appear later in training.

Name: Best Combined
Noted Behavior: Candidate best mix of faster convergence and stable gradients; intended to produce the strongest final reward.
